purpose: Legacy Portal 1-style runtime controller adapted for Portal 2 data. On DOMContentLoaded it reads ?autoloop=1 / ?random=1 / ?textcolor= URL params, runs applyTextColor() to set the A5 text-color mode (adds body.color-cycle / body.color-rainbow class, or writes :root --text-color hex), applies the Enrichment Center Credits override (window.credits + useNaturalDelay/customCreditsLoop flags) via localStorage, then calls applyCustomAudio() which asynchronously looks up an IndexedDB-stored audio blob and forwards it as srcOverride to cake.initMusicPlayer (or null for the bundled Want You Gone.mp3). Splash dismissal sets cake.userReady; canplaythrough sets cake.audioReady; cake.tryStart calls cake.init only when both are true. cake.init draws text borders, schedules audio play via cake.audioStartDelay (0 ms here since Want You Gone has no intro instrumental), types lyrics and credits, and blinks cursors. When custom credits are present and under 2,500 chars, credits type at the natural 33 ms/char pace; in loop mode the credit column wipes and re-types continuously. When custom audio is present, cake.creditsMaxTime is overwritten on loadedmetadata with cake.player.duration so the STRETCH math adapts to the new song length. On audio.ended under ?autoloop=1, an 8 s pause precedes either location.reload() or location.assign('../../launcher/?random=1') — note the double up-level because this variant lives at portal2/portal1style/.
invariants: cake.init must run after the HTML containers exist; it draws borders before scheduling line rendering.
processLyricLine and processCreditLine assume global lyrics and credits arrays are loaded before this file.
creditsMaxTime and creditsStartTime determine the full credit scroll pacing from total character count.
Border character counts in drawLyricsBorder and drawCreditsBorder must stay aligned with the CSS box widths.

cake.userReady / cake.audioReady gate cake.init via cake.tryStart — same pattern as portal2/cake.js. userReady flips from splash dismissal (click, Space, or 5s auto-dismiss under ?autoloop=1); audioReady flips from canplaythrough (or the audio-unavailable fallback).

cake.audioStartDelay = 0 by default — Want You Gone has no intro instrumental, so audio plays at the same moment cake.init runs (in contrast to portal/cake.js where the field is 6.87 s). Field is still surfaced so future per-variant adjustments don't need a refactor.

?autoloop=1 sets cake.autoloop and arms the 5s splash auto-dismiss plus the audio 'ended' listener; ?random=1 sets cake.random and switches the post-credits transition from location.reload() to location.assign('../../launcher/?random=1') — note ../../ since this variant is nested two levels deep.

applyTextColor runs FIRST inside DOMContentLoaded (before applyCustomCredits). It reads URLSearchParams.get('textcolor') and either adds body.color-cycle / body.color-rainbow class or sets :root --text-color to a hex; 'default' and invalid values no-op. CSS in portal2/portal1style/style.css is the load-bearing other half. Identical to portal/cake.js — both variants share the contract.

applyCustomCredits runs inside DOMContentLoaded before applyCustomAudio; it overwrites window.credits when localStorage.aperture.customCredits is non-empty after trim, and arms cake.useNaturalDelay + cake.customCreditsLoop only when length < 2500.

applyCustomAudio runs inside DOMContentLoaded after applyCustomCredits. It opens IndexedDB ('aperture' DB, 'audio' object store) and reads the 'customAudio' record. If present and record.blob is set, URL.createObjectURL produces a blob URL that's forwarded to cake.initMusicPlayer; otherwise cake.initMusicPlayer is called with no override (bundled Want You Gone.mp3). A 1.5 s fallback timer in loadCustomAudio guarantees cake.initMusicPlayer is invoked even if IDB hangs.

cake.initMusicPlayer accepts an optional srcOverride argument. When srcOverride is truthy, a loadedmetadata listener overwrites cake.creditsMaxTime with cake.player.duration so the existing STRETCH math adapts to the new song length. When falsy (bundled audio), creditsMaxTime stays at its hardcoded 144 — preserving original Want You Gone pacing exactly.

cake.naturalCreditsDelay = 33 (ms/char) is the Portal 2 natural pace; processCreditLines uses it when cake.useNaturalDelay is true and otherwise falls through to the STRETCH formula creditsMaxTime * 1000 / totalchars.

cake.restartCustomCredits is invoked via setTimeout from processCreditLines only when cake.customCreditsLoop is true; it wipes the credits column (preserving the blinker DOM node + its blink timer), resets lastCreditsIndex, re-runs initCredits, and re-calls processCreditLines.

IndexedDB schema is shared with the launcher: database 'aperture' version 1, object store 'audio' (no keyPath), key 'customAudio' holding { blob, filename, durationSec }. Both this file and launcher/launcher.js call indexedDB.open with the same upgrade handler so the schema is created once per origin.
gotchas: The file body is wrapped in an IIFE with 'use strict'; cake is exposed to the page via window.cake = cake.

initMusicPlayer is called from inside applyCustomAudio's IDB callback, NOT directly from DOMContentLoaded. The IDB lookup adds <50 ms typical latency; a 1.5 s fallback timer in loadCustomAudio guards against pathological IDB hangs.

If canplaythrough never fires (e.g. corrupted audio source), cake.audioReady stays false and cake.init never runs — the page sits past the splash silently. The audio-unavailable else branch sets audioReady = true so the page at least dismisses past the splash.
setTimeout uses the 3-argument form (callback, delay, ...args) to capture loop-iteration values at schedule time so var-scoped loop variables do not produce the closure trap.
The DOMContentLoaded handler assumes index.html provides an element with id 'splash'; without it splash.addEventListener throws and the credits never start.
Launcher path is ../../launcher/ (two levels up), not ../launcher/ — diverges from portal/ and portal2/ which only need one level. Easy to miscopy.
URL.createObjectURL produces a blob URL that's only valid for the page lifetime; on autoloop reload the URL is regenerated from a fresh IDB read (the blob itself persists across reloads).
The loadedmetadata listener is bound ONLY when srcOverride is truthy. Binding it for bundled audio too would risk overwriting cake.creditsMaxTime with a tiny audio.duration drift.
On autoloop cycle 2+, location.reload() loses the original user gesture; WebView2/browser autoplay policy may block playback (see PHASE6_DESKTOP_APP_PLAN.md risk section).

applyCustomCredits and loadCustomAudio both wrap their storage access in try/catch — Safari private mode and storage-quota errors silently skip the override; the variant then plays the original Portal credits and bundled audio.

restartCustomCredits depends on cake.creditsBlinker being a long-lived DOM node whose blink setTimeout chain captures the same reference. The wipe sets creditsdiv.innerHTML='' which detaches the blinker, then appendChild re-attaches it — the in-flight blink timer survives because it holds the captured reference.

key_functions.name: tryStart
key_functions.summary: Gate function — calls cake.init only when both cake.userReady (splash dismissal) and cake.audioReady (canplaythrough) are true.

key_functions.name: init
key_functions.summary: Caches DOM containers, draws text borders, initializes credits and blinkers, schedules audio play via cake.audioStartDelay, then schedules rendering.

key_functions.name: initMusicPlayer
key_functions.summary: Creates the audio element, points it at srcOverride or 'Want You Gone.mp3', binds loadedmetadata (when overriding, sets cake.creditsMaxTime = cake.player.duration), canplaythrough (sets audioReady + tryStart), and ended listeners, then triggers load().

key_functions.name: processLyricLine
key_functions.summary: Creates lyric spans in chronological order, handles clear markers, and schedules per-character typing.

key_functions.name: processCreditLine
key_functions.summary: Maintains the visible credit window and schedules per-character credit typing.

key_functions.name: processCreditLines
key_functions.summary: Computes per-character delay (natural 33ms when useNaturalDelay, otherwise STRETCH math), schedules every credit line for typing, and arms restartCustomCredits when customCreditsLoop is set.

key_functions.name: restartCustomCredits
key_functions.summary: Custom-credits loop hook: wipes credit-line divs (keeping the blinker), resets lastCreditsIndex, re-runs initCredits, re-appends the blinker, and calls processCreditLines.

key_functions.name: applyTextColor
key_functions.summary: Module-scope helper run FIRST in DOMContentLoaded. Reads URLSearchParams.get('textcolor'); applies body.color-cycle or body.color-rainbow class, or writes :root --text-color hex. No-op on 'default' or invalid. Identical implementation to portal/cake.js.

key_functions.name: applyCustomCredits
key_functions.summary: Module-scope helper run in DOMContentLoaded; reads localStorage.aperture.customCredits / aperture.creditsMode, replaces window.credits, and arms useNaturalDelay + customCreditsLoop when under 2,500 chars.

key_functions.name: loadCustomAudio
key_functions.summary: Module-scope IDB helper. Opens database 'aperture' v1, reads the 'audio' object store's 'customAudio' record, and invokes the callback with either URL.createObjectURL(record.blob) or null. 1.5 s fallback timer guards against IDB hangs.

key_functions.name: applyCustomAudio
key_functions.summary: Module-scope helper run in DOMContentLoaded after applyCustomCredits. Calls loadCustomAudio; on blob URL hit, forwards the URL to cake.initMusicPlayer as srcOverride. On miss, calls cake.initMusicPlayer with no override (bundled audio).
